blocks.content:
Welcome to the create a silence learning path. You'll suppress alert notifications for a specific time window using label matchers.

Silences are ad-hoc suppressions. They stop notifications from being delivered without affecting alert evaluation. Use them for planned maintenance, deployments, or known incidents where you don't need additional notifications.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Navigate to the Silences page
- Create a new silence with label matchers
- Verify which alert instances are affected
- Save and confirm the silence is active

## Before you begin

Before you start, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- At least one alert rule already created
- An understanding of which labels identify the alerts you want to silence
- Editor or Admin role (required to create silences)

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.
